key_insight: HPG-Diff combines hierarchical physics-guided diffusion with a differentiable connectivity penalty to generate topology designs that are both more physically consistent and less prone to floating material.
engineering_relevance: It improves robustness for topology optimization under unseen boundary conditions, which is important for practical lightweight structural design workflows.
method: Aligns multi-level precomputed physics features with the diffusion denoising process
Uses a virtual heat-propagation-based floating material suppression loss to enforce connectivity
Supports adaptation to new rectangular domains with lightweight LoRA fine-tuning
evidence_signal: The abstract reports average compliance errors of 0.87% in-distribution and 5.29% out-of-distribution, with floating material ratios reduced to 2.90% and 2.44%, respectively.
watch_item: The approach depends on precomputed physics features and a differentiable surrogate connectivity constraint, so performance may be sensitive to feature quality and transfer to more complex geometries.
paper_title: HPG-Diff: Hierarchical physics-guided diffusion with differentiable connectivity constraints for topology optimization
